title: WitnessManifest
description: Signed root document for a Gemma.Witness bundle. The Ed25519 signature stored in signature.json covers the RFC 8785 JCS canonicalization of this object.

allOf.description: v1 bundles only allow ed25519 and forbid the hardware-key attestation blob. This keeps every shipped v1 bundle round-trippable while preventing a v1 verifier from being asked to render a field it does not understand.

amends.description: Optional pointer at the bundle this one supersedes. Present only on amendment bundles. The verifier displays the reference; it does not merge bundles or hide the original. Verifiers MUST check that the amending bundle's signer.key_id matches original_signer_key_id and refuse to treat the bundle as a continuation of the chain on mismatch.

original_signer_key_id.description: signer.key_id of the original bundle. Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the original's raw 32-byte public key. The amending bundle's signer.key_id must equal this value for the amendment chain to be trusted.

manifest_version.description: Integer schema version. The verifier routes on this; bump on every incompatible change. v1 bundles use Ed25519-only software signing. v2 widens signer.algorithm to allow ecdsa-p256 and adds an optional signer.attestation blob for hardware-backed key provenance.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which the manifest was sealed.

algorithm.description: Signing algorithm used to produce signature.json over the canonicalized manifest. v1 manifests are restricted to ed25519. v2 manifests may also use ecdsa-p256 (Secure Enclave on macOS, TPM 2.0 on Linux, NCrypt on Windows).

public_key_pem.description: SubjectPublicKeyInfo PEM. Ed25519 keys use the standard PKCS#8 SPKI form; ECDSA P-256 keys use the SECG/RFC 5480 SPKI form.

key_id.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the raw public-key bytes. For Ed25519 this is SHA-256 of the 32-byte point; for ECDSA P-256 it is SHA-256 of the 65-byte uncompressed-point encoding (0x04 || X || Y).

attestation.description: Optional hardware-backed key provenance. Present only on v2 manifests signed by a Secure Enclave / TPM / NCrypt key that can produce an attestation chain. Verifiers display the blob; they do not gate on it without an explicit pinning policy.

format.description: Identifier of the attestation document format. Known values: apple-sep-v1 (Secure Enclave SEP attestation), tpm2-quote-v1 (TPM 2.0 quote+certify pair), ncrypt-v1 (Windows platform-key attestation).

payload_b64.description: Base64 of the attestation payload bytes. Format-specific; opaque to the verifier core.

certificate_chain_b64.description: Optional base64-encoded DER X.509 chain rooting the attestation, leaf-first.

assets.description: Every binary file inside the bundle, keyed by its in-zip path. Hash is SHA-256 of the raw file bytes.

assertions.description: Namespaced assertions about the captured evidence.

gemma.witness.audio_fingerprint.description: Optional advisory perceptual fingerprint of the captured audio. Not a security primitive; the SHA-256 in the audio asset entry remains the cryptographic guarantee. Verifiers display the field and may offer an opt-in re-derivation check.

gemma.witness.inference_parameters.description: Optional advisory assertion recording the sampling parameters used by each inference pass. Forensic, not cryptographic; verifiers display it without passing or failing on its contents.

passes.description: Per-pass parameters keyed by pass name (transcribe, structure_incident, analyze_image, check_consistency).

sampling_seed.description: RNG seed if pinned. Null today across every pass.
